ud8.9:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.9:1.3: Then Ven. Dabba Mallaputta went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud8.9:1.4: As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud8.9:1.5: “Now is the time for my total unbinding, O One-Well-Gone!”
ud8.9:1.6: “Then do, Dabba, what you think it is now time to do.”
ud8.9:2.1:
Then Ven. Dabba Mallaputta, rising from his seat, bowed down to the Blessed One and,
circling him on the right, rose up into the air and sat cross-legged in the sky, in space.
Entering & emerging from the fire property, he was totally unbound.
ud8.9:3.1:
Now, when Dabba Mallaputta rose up into the air and, sitting cross-legged in the sky, in space,
entered & emerged from the fire property and was totally unbound,
his body burned and was consumed so that neither ashes nor soot could be discerned.
ud8.9:3.2: Just as when ghee or oil is burned and consumed, neither ashes nor soot can be discerned,
ud8.9:3.3:
in the same way, when Dabba Mallaputta rose up into the air and, sitting cross-legged in the sky, in space,
entered & emerged from the fire property and was totally unbound,
his body burned and was consumed so that neither ashes nor soot could be discerned.
ud8.9:4.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.9:5.1: The body broke up, perception ceased,
ud8.9:5.4: consciousness has come to an end.
